provenance: Directional priors informed by widely-reported public short-form guidance (fast hooks, dense pacing, clear payoff, end-of-clip retention). These are DESIGN PARAMETERS, not statistics learned from a proprietary dataset. Lantern ships with zero collected rows (see data/creator-intelligence). Weights and thresholds are recalibrated against real collected outcomes when those exist; until then every produced score is a structural measurement of the user's own uploaded video, never a claim about the wider population.

hookSpeed.note: Time from clip start to the first detected highlight onset.

sceneDensity.note: Cuts/scene changes per minute; too few feels slow, extreme density adds no further benefit.

motionEnergy.note: Frame-difference motion magnitude. Used as ONE feature only (per spec), not the whole score.

audioEnergy.note: Audio loudness and its variation; drives emotion and caption-potential proxies.

surprise.note: Density of moments where motion + audio (+scene) spike together — the structural signature of a surprising beat.

rewatch.note: Strength of a highlight near the end (unexpected ending / payoff) plus close-call density.

disclaimer: viralScore is a per-clip STRUCTURAL score. It does not predict real-world views. Population-calibrated scoring lives in scoring/score-engine.js and returns insufficient_data until a dataset exists.
